title: Generics 1
description: Generic functions work with multiple types using type parameters. This avoids code duplication.
instructions: Fix the shopping list by using a generic function instead of duplicating code.
hints: Vec<T> is already a generic type - it works with any type T.
The push method accepts values of type T when the Vec is Vec<T>.
This code should compile as-is. Observe how Vec works with different types.
explanation: Generics allow a single type or function definition to work with many concrete types. `Vec<T>` is a generic type - when you write `Vec<&str>`, T is replaced with `&str`. When you write `Vec<i32>`, T is replaced with `i32`. Rust uses monomorphization to generate specialized code for each concrete type at compile time, so there's no runtime cost.
